text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Egg intake improves carotenoid status by increasing plasma HDL cholesterol in adults with metabolic syndrome.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Egg intake improves carotenoid status by increasing plasma HDL cholesterol in adults with metabolic syndrome.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that egg intake leads to improved carotenoid status through higher plasma HDL cholesterol in adults with metabolic syndrome.","population":"adults with metabolic syndrome","intervention":"egg intake","comparator":"","outcome":"carotenoid status, plasma HDL cholesterol","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Whole egg consumption improves lipoprotein profiles and insulin sensitivity to a greater extent than yolk-free egg substitute in individuals with metabolic syndrome.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Whole egg consumption improves lipoprotein profiles and insulin sensitivity to a greater extent than yolk-free egg substitute in individuals with metabolic syndrome.","entailment":"The sentence reports that whole egg consumption leads to better lipoprotein profiles and insulin sensitivity compared with a yolk‑free egg substitute in people with metabolic syndrome.","population":"individuals with metabolic syndrome","intervention":"whole egg consumption","comparator":"yolk-free egg substitute","outcome":"lipoprotein profiles, insulin sensitivity","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Eggs improve plasma biomarkers in patients with metabolic syndrome following a plant-based diet.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs Improve Plasma Biomarkers in Patients with Metabolic Syndrome Following a Plant-Based Diet-A Randomized Crossover Study.","entailment":"The title of the study indicates that consuming eggs led to improvements in plasma biomarkers among metabolic‑syndrome patients on a plant‑based diet.","population":"patients with metabolic syndrome following a plant‑based diet","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"plasma biomarkers","timeframe":"randomized crossover study","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Eggs in a plant-based diet affect oxidative stress and inflammation in metabolic syndrome.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The Effects of Eggs in a Plant-Based Diet on Oxidative Stress and Inflammation in Metabolic Syndrome.","entailment":"The article title states that eggs, when part of a plant‑based diet, have effects on oxidative stress and inflammation among those with metabolic syndrome.","population":"individuals with metabolic syndrome on a plant‑based diet","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"oxidative stress, inflammation","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg intake versus choline supplementation impacts gut microbiota and plasma carotenoids in subjects with metabolic syndrome.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Comparison between Egg Intake versus Choline Supplementation on Gut Microbiota and Plasma Carotenoids in Subjects with Metabolic Syndrome.","entailment":"The study compares egg intake to choline supplementation and reports differences in gut microbiota and plasma carotenoids among metabolic‑syndrome subjects.","population":"subjects with metabolic syndrome","intervention":"egg intake","comparator":"choline supplementation","outcome":"gut microbiota, plasma carotenoids","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Dietary choline supplements, but not eggs, raise fasting TMAO levels in participants with normal renal function.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Dietary Choline Supplements, but Not Eggs, Raise Fasting TMAO Levels in Participants with Normal Renal Function: A Randomized Clinical Trial.","entailment":"The trial title reports that choline supplements increase fasting TMAO levels, whereas egg consumption does not, in participants with normal kidney function.","population":"participants with normal renal function","intervention":"dietary choline supplements","comparator":"eggs","outcome":"fasting TMAO levels","timeframe":"randomized clinical trial","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"A high‑egg diet affects cardiometabolic risk factors in people with type 2 diabetes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Effect of a high-egg diet on cardiometabolic risk factors in people with type 2 diabetes: The Diabetes and Egg (DIABEGG) Study—randomized weight-loss and follow-up phase.","entailment":"The study title indicates that consuming a high‑egg diet influences cardiometabolic risk factors among individuals with type 2 diabetes.","population":"people with type 2 diabetes","intervention":"high‑egg diet","comparator":"","outcome":"cardiometabolic risk factors","timeframe":"randomized weight‑loss and follow‑up phase","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"}]}
